senses.gloss: statue, statuette, especially a painted idol made of ivory or marble

examples.text: وجمع ناشطون نحو 18 ألف جنيه استرليني لشراء دمية تنفخ بغاز الهيليوم طولها 6 أمتار، ويقولون إنها تجسد شخصية ترامب بأنه "مزاجي كالأطفال، ومغرور".
examples.english: Campaigners raised almost £18,000 for the helium-filled six-metre high figure, which they said reflects Mr Trump's character as an "angry baby with a fragile ego and tiny hands".

etymology.text:
* From د م ي (d m y) of دَم (dam, “blood”), deriving from the red ochre paint used to decorate idols, which is suggested by Safaitic 𐪕𐪃𐪚 (dmy /⁠damaya⁠/, “to draw”); alternatively, in reference to the blood sacrifices as a means to appease them. It has the appearance of آدم (ʔādam, “man”), Ugaritic 𐎀𐎄𐎎 (ảdm, “man”) etc.
* Phonologically dubiously and bare of factual basis, alleging that “it has not a spoor of derivation in Arabic”, Fraenkel claimed it being borrowed from Classical Syriac ܕܘܽܡܳܝܳܐ m (dummāyā), Aramaic דְּמָאִין m pl (dmāʾīn, “likenesses, simulacra”), דְּמוּ m (dmū, “likeness, simulacrum”), דְּמוּתָא f (dmūṯā, “likeness, simulacrum”), related to דְּמָא (dmaʾ, “to be similar”), דְּמֵי (dmey, “to be similar”), Classical Syriac ܕܡܳܐ (dmāʾ, “to be similar”), apparently Arabic دَنَا (danā, “to be near”).

body.text: in which is redness like blood:
(Kr, M, K:) accord. to Abu-I-'Alà, because originally painted with red, as though from
: and any beautiful female is likened thereto, because adorned: (TA:) metonymically applied to (tropical:)
anything that is deemed beautiful in respect of whiteness:

(Lth, S, K:) said in the R to be so called because of the shedding of blood at the place thereof for the purpose of propitiation; but MF says that this derivation requires consideration: more probably because it is decorated: (TA:) pl.
. (S, Mgh, K.) Accord. to MF, it is also pronounced ↓
More beautiful than the image of ivory.
is an oath of the Pagan Arabs, meaning
or, as some relate, it is ↓
of what is sacrificed upon the stones set up to be worshipped: so in the Nh. (TA.) ―         -b2-  The pl.,
